docs.title: WWW::Playwright
docs.text: A Raku driver for Playwright. Raku owns the API surface and lifecycle. Node owns the browser.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
Raku spawns a long-lived Node process, the sidecar, that imports the official playwright npm package. Raku and the sidecar talk over newline-delimited JSON-RPC on stdio.

+-----------+        NDJSON / stdio        +--------------+        +---------+
|   Raku    | ---------------------------> | Node sidecar | -----> | Browser |
| (driver)  | <--------------------------- | (playwright) | <----- |         |
+-----------+        JSON-RPC 2.0          +--------------+        +---------+
  • Raku sends one JSON-RPC request per line and reads one response per line.
  • The sidecar dispatches each request to Playwright and replies on the same line-buffered stream.
  • Browser objects (pages, locators, element handles, contexts) never cross the wire. The sidecar keeps them in a registry and mints opaque string handles (page@1, loc@7) that Raku passes back in later requests.

docs.title: Why a sidecar
docs.text: Playwright ships as a Node library with no stable cross-language RPC surface. Rather than reimplement the protocol, this dist drives the real Node package and keeps Raku as the orchestration layer. Raku starts the sidecar, feeds it requests, correlates responses, and tears it down on exit.

docs.title: Layout
docs.text:
• WWW::Playwright - entry point: start the sidecar, launch a browser, stop.
  • WWW::Playwright::Sidecar - the transport that owns the Node process.
  • WWW::Playwright::Browser, ::Context, ::Page, ::Locator - thin Raku wrappers around sidecar handles.
  • WWW::Playwright::Exception - typed exceptions built from JSON-RPC errors.

docs.title: Action reference
docs.text:
Actions run against a Locator. Get one from $page.locator($selector), then call an action on it. Playwright auto-waits for the element to be actionable before each action.

my $page = $context.new-page;
$page.goto('file:///path/to/hello.html');

docs.title: click
docs.text:
Clicks the element.

$page.locator('#go').click;

docs.title: fill
docs.text:
Sets the value of an input, replacing any existing value.

$page.locator('#name').fill('Ada');

docs.title: type
docs.text:
Types text one key at a time, appending to the current value. Use fill to replace; use type when per-keystroke events matter.

$page.locator('#name').type('Hi');

docs.title: press
docs.text:
Dispatches a single key.

$page.locator('#name').press('Enter');

docs.title: check and uncheck
docs.text:
Tick or clear a checkbox.

$page.locator('#agree').check;
$page.locator('#agree').uncheck;

docs.title: select-option
docs.text:
Selects an option in a <select> by value and returns the chosen values.

my @selected = $page.locator('#color').select-option('green');   # ('green',)

docs.title: hover
docs.text:
Moves the pointer over the element.

$page.locator('#hoverable').hover;

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Two environment variables control how the transport finds Node and how much it reports.

docs.title: PLAYWRIGHT_NODE
docs.text:
The path to the Node binary that runs the sidecar.

When unset, the transport searches PATH for node. When set, it uses that path directly. If the resolved binary does not exist or is not executable, constructing a WWW::Playwright::Sidecar throws X::WWW::Playwright::NodeNotFound with a message pointing at this variable.

PLAYWRIGHT_NODE=/opt/node/bin/node raku your-script.raku

docs.title: PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG
docs.text:
When set to any non-empty value, the transport streams the sidecar's stderr to the Raku process stderr, each line prefixed with [playwright-sidecar].

PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG=1 raku your-script.raku

The sidecar's stderr is always captured and available through $sidecar.stderr-lines, regardless of this flag. PLAYWRIGHT_DEBUG only controls whether those lines are also echoed live.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text: This walks through launching a browser, opening a page, navigating to a local file, and tearing everything down.

docs.title: Start the driver
docs.text:
WWW::Playwright.start spawns the Node sidecar and returns a driver handle.

use WWW::Playwright;

my $playwright = WWW::Playwright.start;

docs.title: Launch a browser
docs.text:
launch starts Chromium. It is headless by default; pass :!headless to see a window.

my $browser = $playwright.launch;          # headless
# my $browser = $playwright.launch(:!headless);

docs.title: Open a context and a page
docs.text:
A context is an isolated browser session, the right boundary for one test. Each context owns its own pages.

my $context = $browser.new-context;
my $page    = $context.new-page;

docs.title: Navigate
docs.text:
goto returns the HTTP status of the navigation response.

my $status = $page.goto('file:///path/to/hello.html');
say $status;   # 200

docs.title: Tear down
docs.text:
Close from the inside out, then stop the sidecar.

$page.close;
$context.close;
$browser.close;

$playwright.stop;

stop closes the sidecar's stdin, which closes any browser the sidecar still holds before the Node process exits.

docs.title: Query reference
docs.text: Queries read state from a Locator. They return values rather than mutating the page.

docs.title: text-content
docs.text:
The element's textContent.

$page.locator('#greeting').text-content;   # 'Hello, world'

docs.title: inner-text
docs.text:
The rendered innerText, reflecting visibility and CSS.

$page.locator('#greeting').inner-text;      # 'Hello, world'

docs.title: get-attribute
docs.text:
The value of a named attribute, or the type object when absent.

$page.locator('#name').get-attribute('type');   # 'text'

docs.title: input-value
docs.text:
The current value of an input, textarea, or select.

$page.locator('#name').input-value;

docs.title: count
docs.text:
The number of elements the locator matches.

$page.locator('#color option').count;       # 3

docs.title: is-visible, is-enabled, is-checked
docs.text:
Boolean state checks.

$page.locator('#greeting').is-visible;       # True
$page.locator('#name').is-enabled;           # True
$page.locator('#agree').is-checked;          # False until checked

docs.title: wait-for
docs.text:
Waits until the element reaches a state: attached, detached, visible, or hidden.

$page.locator('#greeting').wait-for(state => 'visible');

docs.title: Chaining
docs.text:
locator chains from a page or another locator, scoping the selector to the parent.

$page.locator('body').locator('#greeting').text-content;

docs.title: Wire contract
docs.text: Raku and the Node sidecar speak JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio with NDJSON framing. Each message is a single line of compact JSON terminated by \n. The wire is never pretty-printed; a raw newline inside a message would break framing.

docs.title: Messages
docs.text:
Request:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"ping","params":{}}

Success response:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":"pong"}

Failure response:

{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"error":{"code":-32000,"message":"...","data":{"name":"Error","stack":"..."}}}

Every request carries a unique id. The sidecar echoes that id on the matching response so Raku can correlate replies to in-flight calls. Responses may arrive in any order; correlation is by id, not arrival order.

docs.title: Error codes
docs.text:
The sidecar follows the JSON-RPC 2.0 reserved range and adds one implementation-defined code for Playwright failures.

Code Meaning -32700 Parse error: the line was not valid JSON. -32600 Invalid request: missing or non-string method. -32601 Method not found. -32000 A Playwright or handler error was thrown.

A failure response carries the original error name and stack in data so the Raku side can rebuild a typed exception.

docs.title: Handle model
docs.text:
Browser objects never cross the wire. The sidecar keeps a registry mapping opaque string handles to live objects (a Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Locator, or ElementHandle). Handles are minted as kind@N:

browser@1
context@1
page@1
loc@7

A method that creates an object returns its handle. A later request passes the handle back in params.handle (or another named field) and the sidecar resolves it to the live object before acting. Resolving an unknown handle is an error.

docs.title: Methods
docs.text:
Method Params Result ping none "pong" launch { headless: Bool } a browser@N handle close { handle: "kind@N" } null

launch starts Chromium. close closes the object behind a handle and forgets it. When the sidecar's stdin closes, it closes every browser it launched before exiting.
